reviews.review_msg: Dr. Fernando is the sweetest and most caring professor I've ever had, her lectures are informative but fun to listen to. She also really cares about her students and makes an effort to learn their names, would highly recommend!

Mala's course is really hard. There's a lot of information. But she's very funny and fair. I liked going to her office hours because she'd provide hints on specific parts that might be tested. Studying is a must and attendance in labs and lectures are required. But hey, I much prefer a nice, organized professor than a poor one.

This class was heavy in terms of material. But if you really try in labs, try to keep up with the class, and do the projects you will do well. Dr. Fernando gives so many bonus marks, and she's so kind and helpful to her students. She's really my favourite prof at SFU. If you skip class you won't do well. Even if it gets overwhelming, don't skip.

This class was difficult and you really had to put the effort in but she's always willing to answer questions. Her lectures are detailed and informative. I almost failed both the lecture and lab midterms but still got a solid grade in the class by reading the lectures and asking for help.
You need to put in the effort but not a bad prof overall.

Mala is very friendly. She is 100% approachable all the time. However she isn't the best at lecturing. Her lectures are all over the place and aren't very cohesive. The positive side of this is that the exams are based on the lecture slides. The lab sucks, it's hard and the midterm average was 50%. Make up the marks during the lecture part. GL!
shes obsessed with acknowledging indigenous stuff. go teach indig101 then nobody wants to waste their time with that in bisc. waste of time course

Her classes are unique and labs are fairly interesting. However, her final project grading is just deciding your own grade which I found a little strange. The lectures and projects are extremely Indigenous-knowledge heavy, and she sometimes seems to put more importance on Indigenous knowledge than biology.

Miranda is a good prof but I can see why people would disagree with that. The class itself is quite a lot to remember but if you literally stay on top of lectures (eg. review after class) it will be 5x easier
She's a nice person but she doesn't tolerate rude people
Exams were a bit hard but fair game (mostly lecture based+the small details matter).

Miranda is a great professor, labs are fun and straight forward and she is very approachable. My only issues are exams can be a bit knit picky (particularly the final) with images asking you to identify anatomy you likely haven't seen before, so my advice would be to really give it 100% in the labs and truly understanding what you are looking at.

Miranda is clearly passionate about plants and the course. The weekly quizzes aren't too hard, but require pre-reading lectures/labs. The exams are tough, not enough time for the midterm and the final (cumulative) tested on small details, only passable with the cheat sheet (pack this with information and labelled diagrams).
Miranda clearly looks kind and approachable at first but she is evil on the inside when it comes to exams . Tough grader, participation heavy, weekly quizzes.

Good class, requires participation and has weekly quizzes. Exams are fair, labs are very (and imo too) structured. Classes are chill and her slides have most of the info you'll need. Has a high-school/late elementary school style of teaching.

Amazing prof, cares a lot about what students learn, makes material very easy to understand. Her style is participation heavy. I'm personally not a fan of consistent participation, so I didn't enjoy that, but if you do you'll absolutely enjoy the class.

She's a good and caring prof though I find her activities pointless sometimes as others have said a lot. Lecture slides are good and have lots of information. Exams are definitely fair but time crunched and I'm not a fan of the weekly quizzes. She kind of has a high school style of teaching if you like that. Imo, BISC 326 lab is her best class.
